perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of five (5) years from the date hereof or until such time as all of the Proprietary Information becomes subject to any of the exceptions set forth in Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof or until such time as all of the Proprietary Information becomes subject to any of the exceptions set forth in Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the term of the agreement from 'five (5) years' to 'two (2) years.' This directly contradicts Section 12, which implicitly assumes the original five-year term by not placing any earlier limitations on the agreement's duration, thus creating uncertainty about the intended length of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning the matters discussed herein and supersedes and replaces all prior agreements, understandings, writings and discussions between the parties, with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument executed by authorized representatives of the parties. This Agreement shall not create any obligation for either party to enter into any agreement or relationship with the other. Either party may end discussions of a possible relationship at any time and for any or no reason and each party reserves the right to disclose its own confidential information to any third party at any time. This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterpart copies, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which shall together be deemed to constitute one Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from 'five (5) years' to 'two (2) years'. While the contradicted text doesn't explicitly mention a five-year term, the explanation suggests that it implicitly assumes the original five-year term. By reducing the term to two years, a direct inconsistency is created regarding the agreement's intended duration. The contradiction falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections now imply conflicting durations, leading to uncertainty about the contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement, excluding however, any information related to marketing plans.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the definition of 'Proprietary Information' in Section 1 to explicitly exclude 'any information related to marketing plans'. This contradicts the original definition, which included marketing plans, and also contradicts Section 2, which prohibits the use or disclosure of the other party's Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the other party. Now there's uncertainty regarding the protection of marketing plan information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, at any time, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Proprietary Information' to exclude 'any information related to marketing plans.' The original text included marketing plans within the definition of Proprietary Information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that OCERA and COMPANY will not use or disclose the other party's Proprietary Information without prior written consent. Since marketing plans are no longer considered Proprietary Information under the changed definition, there is now a contradiction regarding whether the use or disclosure of marketing plans requires prior written consent.

perturbation.original_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, at any time, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, at any time, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose. However, COMPANY may disclose OCERA's Proprietary Information to its financial advisors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a clause in Section 2 that 'COMPANY may disclose OCERA's Proprietary Information to its financial advisors'. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the same section, which prohibits the use or disclosure of the other party's Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of the other party. The exception creates uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, at any time, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose. However, COMPANY may disclose OCERA's Proprietary Information to its financial advisors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original confidentiality agreement. The original text states that neither party can disclose the other's Proprietary Information without prior written consent. The changed text then adds that COMPANY can disclose OCERA's Proprietary Information to its financial advisors, without OCERA's consent. This directly contradicts the initial agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, indicating that the contradiction exists within the same section of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto shall take reasonable and necessary measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information, including, without limitation, taking such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto shall take reasonable measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information. The measures taken need not be as strict as those used to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 1 by weakening the standard of care for protecting Proprietary Information. Originally, each party had to take 'reasonable and necessary measures, including such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential information.' The changed text removes 'necessary' and clarifies that the measures 'need not be as strict,' which now contradicts the earlier intention of equal protection. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the actual level of protection required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preservation of Confidentiality . OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement. Each party hereto shall take reasonable measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information. The measures taken need not be as strict as those used to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information. Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the standard of care for protecting Proprietary Information, stating that 'The measures taken need not be as strict as those used to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information.' This directly contradicts the original intention implied within the contradicted text's first paragraph that intends for OCERA and COMPANY to 'maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data... of the other party', creating uncertainty about the level of protection required.

perturbation.original_text: Upon conclusion or termination of the discussions between the parties regarding the Purpose, or forthwith upon the request of the other party, OCERA and COMPANY shall promptly destroy or return to the other party all such documents, drawings, electronic data or reproductions thereof which may have come into its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each retain one (1) copy of such information in their confidential files solely for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copy remaining subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon conclusion or termination of the discussions between the parties regarding the Purpose, or forthwith upon the request of the other party, OCERA and COMPANY shall promptly destroy or return to the other party all such documents, drawings, electronic data or reproductions thereof which may have come into its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each retain multiple copies of such information in their confidential files solely for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copy remaining subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 3 by changing the number of copies that can be retained. Originally the text says 'one (1) copy', and it was replaced by 'multiple copies'. The uncertainty on how many copies is going to be retained leads to confusion, and legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose, OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will make no copies, photocopies, facsimiles, or other reproductions of any documents, drawings, electronic data or the like containing the other party’s Proprietary Information. Upon conclusion or termination of the discussions between the parties regarding the Purpose, or forthwith upon the request of the other party, OCERA and COMPANY shall promptly destroy or return to the other party all such documents, drawings, electronic data or reproductions thereof which may have come into its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each retain multiple copies of such information in their confidential files solely for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copy remaining subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the number of copies that OCERA and COMPANY can retain from 'one (1) copy' to 'multiple copies'. This directly contradicts the earlier statement in the provided text 'Except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose, OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will make no copies, photocopies, facsimiles, or other reproductions of any documents'. The change creates an inconsistency regarding the permitted number of copies, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
